query: Microsoft annual revenue 2025 Form 10-K total revenue fiscal 2025
answer: Microsoft's fiscal 2025 total revenue was $75 billion. The Intelligent Cloud unit led growth, with Azure and cloud services revenue exceeding $29.88 billion. The company projected annual growth above 50%.

results.title: Segment Revenue and Operating Income - FY25 Q4 - Microsoft
results.snippet: | us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerExcludingAssessedTax msft:ProductivityAndBusinessProcessesMember | Productivity and Business Processes | **$ 120,810** | $ 106,820 | $ 94,151 |. | us-gaap:OperatingIncomeLoss msft:ProductivityAndBusinessProcessesMember | Productivity and Business Processes | **$ 69,773** | $ 59,661 | $ 50,074 |. | us-gaap:OperatingIncomeLoss msft:IntelligentCloudMember | Intelligent Cloud | **44,589** | 37,813 | 28,411 |. | us-gaap:OperatingIncomeLoss msft:MorePersonalComputingMember | More Personal Computing | **14,166** | 11,959 | 10,038 |. *IMPORTANT NOTICE TO USERS (summary only,  click here  for full text of notice)* All information is unaudited unless otherwise noted or accompanied by an audit opinion and is subject to the more comprehensive information contained in our SEC reports and filings. All information speaks as of the last fiscal quarter or year for which we have filed a Form 10-K or 10-Q, or for historical information the date or period expressly indicated in or with such information. Information contained in these documents is current as of the earnings date, and not restated for new accounting standards. #### Related Information.

results.title: FY25 Q4 - More Personal Computing Performance - Investor Relations
results.snippet: More Personal Computing. Fiscal Year 2025 Compared with Fiscal Year 2024. Revenue increased $3.8 billion or 7%. • Windows and Devices revenue increased $288

results.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Q4 earnings report 2025 - CNBC
results.snippet: * The company reported revenue from Azure and cloud services for the first time, with sales exceeding $75 billion for fiscal 2025. Microsoft shares jumped 9% in extended trading on Wednesday after the company reported better-than-expected earnings and revenue for the fiscal fourth quarter. Microsoft's finance chief, Amy Hood, called for $74.7 billion to $75.8 billion in fiscal first-quarter revenue. Hood said Microsoft sees over $30 billion in fiscal first quarter capital expenditures and assets acquired through finance leases, which would work out to annual growth above 50%. The company's Intelligent Cloud unit, which includes the Azure cloud, produced $29.88 billion in revenue, up about 26% and beating the StreetAccount consensus of $28.92 billion. Microsoft had $24.2 billion in capital expenditures and assets acquired through finance leases for the quarter, up 27% from a year earlier. Microsoft said it had $1.71 billion in other expense during the quarter.
